Project.name: Bayesian_Atari_Lunar_Lander_ALE
Project.Overview: An autonomous agent for the raw Atari 2600 Lunar Lander ROM using ale-py. The project bypasses Gymnasium and Deep RL libraries to implement a "Physics-First" control law. We use Bayesian Inference (Kalman Filter) to estimate continuous state from noisy 8-bit RAM, and a Decision- Theoretic policy to pick actions based on Expected Utility.

Environment.API: from ale_py import ALEInterface, roms ale = ALEInterface() ale.loadROM(roms.get_rom_path("lunar_lander")) ale.reset_game() reward = ale.act(action) # Action Space: 0=NOOP, 1=Left, 2=Main, 3=Right ram = ale.getRAM() # 128-byte state

Observation.Note: RAM values are quantized integers. Kalman Filtering is required to infer smooth continuous physics.

Layer.name: KalmanInference
Layer.Purpose: Treat jumpy RAM bytes as noisy observations Z. Maintain a posterior belief N(mu, P) over the hidden continuous state [y, y_dot, x_dot, angle].
Layer.PredictionStep: x_pred = A · x + b(action) (Newtonian kinematics) P_pred = A · P · A^T + Q
Layer.UpdateStep:
y = z − H · x_pred (innovation) S = H · P_pred · H^T + R (innovation covariance) K = P_pred · H^T · S^{-1} (Kalman Gain) x_upd = x_pred + K · y (posterior mean) P_upd = (I − K · H) · P_pred (posterior covariance)
This IS the Bayesian update: P(State | Obs) ∝ P(Obs | State) · P(State)
Layer.Implementation: class KalmanFilter: reset(z0) predict(action) # prior update(z) # posterior predict_state(action) -> mu # hypothetical (no mutation)

Layer.name: HeuristicPolicy
Layer.Purpose: Pick the action that maximises Expected Utility for the next frame.
Layer.DecisionRule: For each action a in {0,1,2,3}: 1. Run Kalman 'predict_state' to see where the lander goes. 2. U(a) = -w_vy·|y_dot| - w_vx·|x_dot| - w_angle·|angle| - fuel(a). 3. Choose a* = argmax U(a).

Layer.name: ValidationSuite
Layer.Component: Plot Predicted Safety Probability vs. Empirical Landing Success Rate. Brier Score and Expected Calibration Error (ECE).
Sweep threshold on P(Crash) and plot Precision-Recall curve for 'Safe Landing' vs 'Crash' classifications.
Explicit Poisson PMF + Knuth sampler for offline event-rate analysis.
Fit main-engine frame counts per episode to a Negative Binomial distribution via method-of-moments. Estimate P(FuelDepleted).

File.name: config.py
File.desc: All hyperparameters (ALE, RAM addresses, Kalman noise, utility weights).

File.name: env_wrapper.py
File.desc: Wrapper around ALEInterface; extracts RAM bytes to float observations.

File.name: kalman_filter.py
File.desc: 4-D Kalman filter: predict + update. Explicit P(S|O) logic.

File.name: bayes_agent.py
File.desc: Agent: Kalman state estimation + utility-based action selection.

File.name: fuel_model.py
File.desc: Negative Binomial fit and PMF from scratch.

File.name: train.py
File.desc: Main loop: episodes, logging, metrics, plots.

Project.Constraint: - NO PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-Learn. - All Bayesian updates and matrix math (Kalman) must be implemented using NumPy only. - Must explicitly show the P(S | O) update logic in the code.
